cf_parsed.subtask_body:
I re-checked the incumbent by looking for obvious 2-opt/local reorder gains in the two weak areas: the bottom-right outlier 11 and the left chain {13,4,18,16,3}.
The strongest improvement found is to move city 11 from between 9 and 19 to the very end of the tour, attached to 3 and 9.
New tour: 3-14-2-7-9-19-6-0-8-5-10-1-17-15-12-13-4-18-16-11-(back to 3).
Why this helps: it replaces 9-11 and 11-19 plus 16-3 with 9-19, 16-11, 11-3.
Approx lengths:
old pieces: 9-11≈47, 11-19≈56, 16-3≈50 => about 153.
new pieces: 9-19≈32, 16-11≈95, 11-3≈83 => about 210, so that move is worse.
I also tested placing 11 between 3 and 14:
replace 16-3, 14-2 with 16-11, 11-3, 14-2 or variants; clearly worse due to long 16-11 and 11-3 edges.
Tested swapping left-cluster order toward ...13-12-15-17... instead of ...17-15-12-13...; worse because 13 is naturally linked downward to 4, while 17 is naturally linked to 1.
Tested ...4-16-18-13... style reorderings; worse because 13-4 is already short and 18-16 is also short.
Tested whether 10 should precede 5 or 1 directly; current 5-10-1 remains best among those three.
Tested replacing 0-8-5 with 0-5-8; worse since 0 and 8 are adjacent almost exactly.
Conclusion: no local move I checked beats the incumbent; prior tour still looks like the best found.
cf_parsed.updated_plan_state: Best known tour remains the noncrossing geometric sweep of length about 443. Extra work would be a broader 2-opt/3-opt style search, but the most obvious local changes did not improve it.

parsed.subtask_body:
I built a noncrossing geometric tour by sweeping the left cluster upward, crossing to the right/top cluster, then dropping to the bottom-right outlier 11 and returning via the bottom-left.
Candidate kept: 3-14-2-7-9-11-19-6-0-8-5-10-1-17-15-12-13-4-18-16-(back to 3).
Key edge checks suggest this is much shorter than baseline:
3-14≈38, 14-2≈35, 2-7≈1, 7-9≈10, 9-11≈47,
11-19≈56, 19-6≈14, 6-0≈11, 0-8≈1, 8-5≈6,
5-10≈15, 10-1≈23, 1-17≈23, 17-15≈19, 15-12≈19,
12-13≈18, 13-4≈27, 4-18≈19, 18-16≈11, 16-3≈50.
Total is about 443, a huge improvement over 560 baseline.
I checked a few alternatives:
- putting 11 after 9 seems better than attaching 11 near top-right then returning.
- ordering 0-8-5 is clearly right because those three are almost coincident.
- using ...1-17-15-12-13... beats ...1-15-17-12-13... by rough distances.
- left side ...13-4-18-16-3... is cleaner than involving 14 later.
Most likely remaining gains are small local reorderings near {12,13,4,18,16} and whether 10 should sit between 5 and 1 (it should).
No obvious crossings remain in the kept tour.
parsed.updated_plan_state: Have a strong valid tour around length 443 by geometric sweep. One more pass would only test a handful of 2-opt/local reorderings around the left cluster and the 9-11-19 connection, but current answer is already likely near-optimal relative to baseline.

transcript.content:
PLAN_STATE: Use geometry to cluster cities: left chain {3,14,16,18,4,12,13,15}, middle {2,7,9,17}, right/top-right {0,1,5,6,8,10,19,11}. First build a plausible outer-cycle tour with monotone sweep to avoid crossings. Then test a few key reconnections around the right cluster and around links into city 11, which is a likely outlier. Compare against baseline and keep the shortest valid full cycle found. If needed, do one more refinement pass using obvious 2-opt edge swaps and local reorderings in dense clusters. Stop once improvements look marginal relative to time cost.
NEXT_SUB: {"id": 1, "desc": "Construct one improved feasible candidate tour by geometric clustering and crossing elimination, with attention to the right-side cluster and city 11 attachment", "p_solve": 0.42, "time_budget_s": 180}

SUB_1: I built a noncrossing geometric tour by sweeping the left cluster upward, crossing to the right/top cluster, then dropping to the bottom-right outlier 11 and returning via the bottom-left.
Candidate kept: 3-14-2-7-9-11-19-6-0-8-5-10-1-17-15-12-13-4-18-16-(back to 3).
Key edge checks suggest this is much shorter than baseline:
3-14≈38, 14-2≈35, 2-7≈1, 7-9≈10, 9-11≈47,
11-19≈56, 19-6≈14, 6-0≈11, 0-8≈1, 8-5≈6,
5-10≈15, 10-1≈23, 1-17≈23, 17-15≈19, 15-12≈19,
12-13≈18, 13-4≈27, 4-18≈19, 18-16≈11, 16-3≈50.
Total is about 443, a huge improvement over 560 baseline.
I checked a few alternatives:
- putting 11 after 9 seems better than attaching 11 near top-right then returning.
- ordering 0-8-5 is clearly right because those three are almost coincident.
- using ...1-17-15-12-13... beats ...1-15-17-12-13... by rough distances.
- left side ...13-4-18-16-3... is cleaner than involving 14 later.
Most likely remaining gains are small local reorderings near {12,13,4,18,16} and whether 10 should sit between 5 and 1 (it should).
No obvious crossings remain in the kept tour.

BEST_GUESS: {"tour":[3,14,2,7,9,11,19,6,0,8,5,10,1,17,15,12,13,4,18,16]}
UPDATED_PLAN_STATE: Have a strong valid tour around length 443 by geometric sweep. One more pass would only test a handful of 2-opt/local reorderings around the left cluster and the 9-11-19 connection, but current answer is already likely near-optimal relative to baseline.

CONTINUE_FORECAST: {"p_improve":0.28,"expected_delta_score":1.2,"expected_gap_reduction":1.2}
DECISION: stop
